summary: This chunk details the structure and content of conjurations in the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focusing on the nine categories of invocations used to compel spirits—especially divine names, Christological references, and demonic hierarchies. It lists numerous divine and demonic names (e.g., Tetragrammaton, Adonay, Astaroth, Toth, Lucifer) and highlights ritual analogies drawn from biblical narratives (e.g., Jordan standing still, Red Sea parting). Special attention is given to the 'Bond of Satan or Mirage', a conjuration directed at the demon Mirage/Satan in multiple manuscript versions.
key_passages: by Toth, your prince; by Zambrim et Mambrim; by Vsuel [and] by Saduel, to whom you are held to obey and tell the truth
by the power and dignity of Lucifer, Aphaleon and Neutrion
spirits are conjured by ‘all the experiments of Virgil [the necromancer]’
just as God commanded the Jordan and it stood still that the children of Israel might walk across without hindrance, so to I command you to obey my precepts...
